task_completion.description: Create the three required users (REFDESK, CATALOGER, LIBADMIN) with their specified passwords.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the 'Execute SQL Statement' window (Tools -> SQL) showing CREATE USER commands for REFDESK, CATALOGER, and LIBADMIN, or the User Administration GUI showing these three users being added.

task_completion.description: Grant SELECT access to REFDESK on all 11 tables.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for SQL commands granting SELECT on all 11 tables (Artist, Album, Track, Genre, MediaType, Playlist, PlaylistTrack, Customer, Employee, Invoice, InvoiceLine) to REFDESK, or GUI checkboxes showing Read/Select access for all tables.

task_completion.description: Grant SELECT, INSERT, UPDATE, and DELETE permissions to CATALOGER on the 7 content tables.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for SQL commands granting SELECT, INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE on the 7 content tables (Artist, Album, Track, Genre, MediaType, Playlist, PlaylistTrack) to CATALOGER, or GUI checkboxes showing these specific permissions.

task_completion.description: Grant SELECT permission to CATALOGER on the 4 financial/admin tables.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for SQL commands granting SELECT on the 4 admin tables (Customer, Employee, Invoice, InvoiceLine) to CATALOGER, or GUI checkboxes showing only Read/Select access for these tables.

task_completion.description: Grant the DBA role to LIBADMIN.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the SQL command 'GRANT DBA TO LIBADMIN' (or similar) being executed, or a GUI setting assigning the DBA/Admin role to LIBADMIN.

task_completion.description: Save the database to persist the changes.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the agent clicking the Save icon (floppy disk) in the main LibreOffice Base window, or using File -> Save.

integrity.description: Verify that the agent used LibreOffice Base to perform the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: The actions must be performed within the LibreOffice Base application interface, not via a terminal, external database client, or script.

integrity.description: Verify that the SQL commands were actually executed or GUI changes applied.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for success messages such as 'Command successfully executed' in the SQL status area, or confirmation dialogs in the GUI, ensuring commands were not just typed and abandoned.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The Chinook database contains 11 tables. Content tables (7): Artist, Album, Track, Genre, MediaType, Playlist, PlaylistTrack. Admin tables (4): Customer, Employee, Invoice, InvoiceLine. Expected users: REFDESK (password: refdesk2024), CATALOGER (password: catalog2024), LIBADMIN (password: admin2024). In LibreOffice Base, permissions are typically managed via Tools -> SQL using commands like CREATE USER and GRANT, or via the User Administration GUI.
